steps.title: Support the underlined claim
steps.content: The underlined claim says My Name Is Red resembles classic magical realist novels because it juxtaposes literary realism with Turkish folklore. The best quotation must show those two elements working together in the novel, not merely list influences or genres.

steps.title: Require juxtaposition
steps.content: Choice A discusses Turkish folklore and Latin American magical realism as influences, but it does not directly show realism and folklore interacting in the novel. Choice C says the novel is indebted to Turkish folklore, which is too general. Choice D says the novel alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes, but alternation is weaker than the claimed juxtaposition. Choice B directly describes a realistic plot being disrupted by imagery and situations from Turkish folklore, matching the underlined claim. The answer is B.
